seo.title: Red sauce for pasta - Marinda Kook

html:
It's that time of year when I struggle to get myself to switch on my stove. Even unpacking the dishwasher feels like too much effort. So today I decided to make quick food. For the red sauce you just throw everything together and forget about it for at least 60 minutes.

 

YOU NEED:

  • 1 onion
  • 6 garlic cloves
  • 30 ml oil
  • 60 ml brown sugar
  • 2 tins tomato and onion mix
  • 1 tub tomato paste
  • 125 ml red wine (use your best wine)
  • 6 peppercorns
  • 30 ml chilli flakes (or as hot as you'd like it)
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • thyme and rosemary (a sprig of each)

NOW:

  1. slice the onion into rings and fry lightly until soft and golden in the oil
  2. chop the garlic finely and fry it along with the onion
  3. add all the rest of the ingredients and let it simmer for 60 minutes – it gets this deep red/brown colour

We ate it together with pasta. Sprinkle generously with parmesan cheese. Wash it down with a cold glass.
